Class AlertSystem

java.lang.Object
AlertSystem
public class AlertSystem extends Object
Container class that manages notification delivery through composition. AlertSystem uses a NotificationMedium object as a field, allowing the notification mechanism to be swapped at runtime without modifying the core alert logic. This design follows the Composition over Inheritance principle, making the system highly maintainable and extensible. Features: - Dynamic medium switching via setMedium() - Session logging via ArrayList - Timestamp tracking for each notification
Since:
2026-06-09
Version:
1.0
Author:
Phil
See Also:

  • EmailService
  • SMSService

    Constructors

    Constructs a new AlertSystem with no initial medium.

    Constructs a new AlertSystem with an initial notification medium.

  • Constructor Details

    • AlertSystem

      public AlertSystem()
      Constructs a new AlertSystem with no initial medium. A medium must be set before calling notifyUser().

    • AlertSystem

      public AlertSystem(NotificationMedium medium)
      Constructs a new AlertSystem with an initial notification medium.
      Parameters:
      medium - The initial NotificationMedium to use for sending alerts

  • Method Details

    • setMedium

      public void setMedium(NotificationMedium medium)
      Sets or changes the notification medium at runtime. This method demonstrates the power of composition - the AlertSystem can switch between Email, SMS, or any future NotificationMedium implementation without any code changes to AlertSystem itself.
      Parameters:
      medium - The new NotificationMedium to use
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if medium is null

    • notifyUser

      public void notifyUser(String message)
      Sends a notification message using the currently configured medium. The message is logged with a timestamp for session tracking.
      Parameters:
      message - The message content to be delivered
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if no medium has been set
      IllegalArgumentException - if the message is null or empty

    • getMessageLog

      public ArrayList<String> getMessageLog()
      Returns the message log for this session.
      Returns:
      A copy of the ArrayList containing all sent messages

    • getTimestampLog

      public ArrayList<String> getTimestampLog()
      Returns the timestamp log corresponding to each message.
      Returns:
      A copy of the ArrayList containing all timestamps

    • getMessageCount

      public int getMessageCount()
      Returns the number of messages sent in this session.
      Returns:
      The count of logged messages

    • clearLog

      public void clearLog()
      Clears the message and timestamp logs.

    • printLog

      public void printLog()
      Prints the session log to stdout.

    • main

      public static void main(String[] args)
      Main method for CLI execution. Demonstrates the AlertSystem with both Email and SMS services, showing how composition allows easy medium switching.
      Parameters:
      args - Command line arguments (not used)
